title: maranthes aubrevillei
intro: Maranthes aubrevillei, a plant species belonging to the family Chrysobalanaceae, is commonly known as Aubreville's maranthe or African breadfruit. Traditionally, the plant has been used in African medicine, where its roots and leaves are valued for their medicinal properties, particularly for treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's roots and leaves have been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating pain. In herbal preparations, Maranthes aubrevillei is often used in infusions, decoctions, and ointments to treat various health issues.
